hypothesis: If a single score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state data and its reverse SDE is guided by interpolated conditional scores before applying score-aligned ascent along r_SAA = s^B − s^A, then it will generate near-committor-0.5 transition-state configurations and accurate reaction pathways across diverse systems, outperforming linear/geodesic interpolation and SBI- or SAA-only ablations, because the interpolated scores approximate the committor gradient and their difference provides an ascent direction to the saddle while suppressing orthogonal motion.
